stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Dr. Lin Wei studies fossils at the Yunnan University of Paleontology.
  • Wei's research team recently found a 520-million-year-old arthropod fossil.
  • It is the oldest fossil from the arthropod group ever recovered with preserved soft tissue.
  • It was found in the Chengjiang shale beds in southern China.
stem: The student wants to indicate where the arthropod fossil was found. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Wei's team recently found the oldest arthropod fossil with preserved soft tissue ever recovered.
The arthropod fossil was found in the Chengjiang shale beds in southern China.
Dr. Lin Wei, of the Yunnan University of Paleontology, is part of a research team.
The fossil, which is from the arthropod group, is 520 million years old.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The sentence indicates where the arthropod fossil was found: the Chengjiang shale beds in southern China.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence states that Wei's team discovered the arthropod fossil; it doesn't specify where they found it. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence provides information about Dr. Lin Wei; it doesn't indicate where the fossil was found. Choice D is incorrect. While the sentence provides information about the fossil, it doesn't indicate where the fossil was found.
